body:
This PR introduces the precomputation of buoyancy gradients, which are required for most parameterization. 
By doing this, we avoid heavyl interpolation of buoyancy computations (for example in slope computations or in Richardson number computations) speeding up significantly the parameterizations.

labels.description: runs preconfigured benchamarks and spits out timing
labels.name: benchmark performance

title: Materialize buoyancy gradients for use in parameterizations

body:
With @giordano, we are trying to debug the problem encountered in julia 1.11 and 1.12, but we are encountering a very strange issue.  We found a typo that this PR fixes, but now I am incredibly puzzled how the codebase has been working till now.

Edit: the function above was used here.
title: Typo in `BuoyancyFormulations`
